dependencies: Reliance on AWS for cloud infrastructure and exchange migration
Dependency on third-party service providers for data center, cloud computing, data storage and processing, connectivity, data content, clearing, and maintaining markets
Reliance on members of trading community to maintain markets and add liquidity
risks: Service interruptions or delays from third-party providers could impair services
AWS platform outages could affect Nasdaq workloads and client services
Failure of AWS to deliver system requirements on time could delay exchange migration to cloud
Difficulties or failures of largest members could materially affect business
Supply chain vendor cybersecurity breaches could result in collateral damage

regulatory_risks: Extensive regulation by SEC, FINRA, and other SROs in the U.S.
Subject to MiFID II and MiFIR regulations in European Union and European Economic Area
European Union Market Abuse Regulation compliance
Benchmark Regulation compliance required by January 1, 2026
Regulation NMS and Options Intermarket Linkage Plan requirements
Regulation SCI compliance for technology infrastructure
CAT (Consolidated Audit Trail) implementation and cost recovery uncertainties
CFTC regulation of derivatives products
Canadian Securities Administrators oversight and Data Fees Methodology restrictions
DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act) compliance effective January 2025
SFSA (Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority) supervision and potential fines
Financial Conduct Authority regulation in United Kingdom
EMIR (European Market Infrastructure Regulation) compliance for clearing operations
Potential SEC rule changes reducing fees for exchange access and rebates
Economic sanctions compliance (U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control)
Export controls and anti-corruption regulations (Foreign Corrupt Practices Act)
Anti-money laundering compliance requirements
Privacy and data protection laws (GDPR, California Privacy Rights Act, HIPAA, Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act)
Cybersecurity and data sovereignty regulations
Changes in tax laws, regulations, or policies including renewable energy tax credits
Potential federal or state financial transaction taxes
Section 31 fees to SEC for supervision and regulation

sanction_risks: Limitations on securing new listings or entering transactions with customers due to political, economic or social events
Imposition of governmental economic sanctions or tariffs on countries where business is conducted or expansion is planned
Compliance with economic sanctions administered by U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control
Restrictions on international or non-U.S. citizen employees of U.S. companies
